network_input.description: Network socket input — fully attacker-controlled over the wire

cgi_input.description: CGI environment variables — attacker-controlled HTTP request fields

stdin_input.description: Standard input / file reading — attacker-controlled if reading from network pipe or uploaded file
stdin_input.note: Check what fd/FILE* is being read from to determine if truly attacker-controlled

file_input.description: File open — attacker-controlled if path or content is from untrusted source

ipc_input.description: IPC mechanisms — attacker-controlled if shared with untrusted process

user_kernel_boundary.description: Linux kernel user-space input — attacker-controlled from userland

ioctl_input.description: Windows IOCTL input buffer — attacker-controlled from userland

argv.description: Command line arguments — attacker-controlled in some contexts (local privesc)

dbus_input.description: D-Bus message input — attacker-controlled from local user
